titleEn: Ropa introduces automatic steering system for potato harvesters
titleZh: Ropa为马铃薯收获机推出自动转向系统
summaryZh: 德国农机厂商Ropa为其牵引式马铃薯收获机Keiler 2 RK22推出了自动转向系统。该系统可自主控制转向轴，并根据计算出的行驶速度和转向角度自动调整，实现智能跟随，提升了收获作业的自动化水平和精度。
translationZh:
德国机械制造商Ropa为其牵引式Ropa Keiler 2 RK22马铃薯收获机推出了一套自动转向系统。该系统可自主控制转向轴。此外，自动转向系统会计算行驶速度和转向角度，并据此自动调整转向轴。这套“智能牵引转向系统”使收获机能够跟随

德国机械制造商Ropa为其牵引式Ropa Keiler 2 RK22马铃薯收获机推出了一套自动转向系统。该系统可自主控制转向轴。此外，自动转向系统还能计算行驶速度和转向角度，并据此自动调整转向轴。这套“智能牵引转向系统”使收获机能够跟随……
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了自动化技术在种植业收获环节的最新应用，对智慧畜牧行业（尤其是关注大田作物与农机自动化的从业者）具有技术趋势参考价值。
contentFull: Ropa introduces automatic steering system for potato harvesters 01-06 | Smart farming | NewsRopa has introduced an automatic steering system for its trailed Keiler 2 RK22 potato harvesters. The harvester follows the tractor’s track as closely as possible without any input from the operator. Photo: RopaGerman machinery manufacturer Ropa has introduced an automatic steering system for its trailed Ropa Keiler 2 RK22 potato harvester. The system autonomously controls the steered axle.In addition, the automatic steering system calculates the driving speed and steering angle, automatically adjusting the steered axle accordingly. The ‘intelligent trail steering system’ enables the harvester to follow the tractor’s path as accurately as possible. The operator no longer needs to monitor this, reducing fatigue and improving ease of operation.Also works when reversingThe steering system also functions when reversing. Once the automatic trail steering system is activated using a button on the control lever, switching the harvester on and off is controlled via the headland and end-of-row buttons on the joystick. As soon as the machine is engaged in the row, the automatic steering system deactivates itself. When the machine is switched off at the end of the row, the steering system automatically reactivates.Available next seasonThe automatic steering system will be available on Ropa potato harvesters from next season. Pricing has not yet been announced.Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week.
